questions.text: In an investigation, equal amount of water was placed in three test tubes labelled G, H and J. Pond weeds of equal length were dropped in each test tube. The test tubes were then placed in identical conditions of light and Carbon (IV) oxide at different temperature for five minutes. After five minutes the bubbles produced in each test tube were counted for one minute. The results were as shown below. TESTUBE TEMPERATURE C NUMBER OF BUBBLES

subQuestions.text: State two reasons why a pond weed was used in this experiment?

questions.text: In cats, the gene for fur colour is sex linked. Letter G represents the gene for ginger fur colour while letter B represents the gene for black fur colour in a given cat species. These genes are codominant. Page 2 of 9 Heterozygous females have ginger and black patches of fur and their phenotype is described as tortoise shell.

subQuestions.label: a

SECTION A (40 MARKS)
Answer ALL the questions in this section.
1.In an investigation, equal amount of water was placed in three test tubes labelled G, H and J. Pond weeds
of equal length were dropped in each test tube. The test tubes were then placed in identical conditions of
light and Carbon (IV) oxide at different temperature for five minutes. After five minutes the bubbles
produced in each test tube were counted for one minute. The results were as shown below.
TESTUBE TEMPERATURE C NUMBER OF BUBBLES
(i) Name one requirement for this process that is not mentioned in the investigation (1mk)
(i1)) Name the gas produced in this investigation (1mk)
(iii) Account for the results in test tube H (2mks)
(iv)Suggest the number of Bubbles if the experiment was to be carried out at 70° C, Give a reason for
your answer (2 mks)
(v) State two reasons why a pond weed was used in this experiment? (2 mks)
2. In cats, the gene for fur colour is sex linked. Letter G represents the gene for ginger fur colour while
letter B represents the gene for black fur colour in a given cat species. These genes are codominant.
Page 2 of 9

Heterozygous females have ginger and black patches of fur and their phenotype is described as
tortoise shell.
a) With reference to the information given above, what is meant by the term codominance. (1mk)
b) Explain why male cats with tortoise-shell phenotype do not usually occur (2 mks)
c) A tortoise shell female was crossed with a black male. Using a Punnet square determine the
genotype and phenotypes of the offsprings. (5 mks)
